meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't monetize skills (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't monetize skills. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Scale Your Freelance Biz with AI
content.seo_description: Stop struggling to monetize dev skills. Learn to build high-margin SaaS products rapidly using Antigravity and Claude Code in this advanced freelancer guide.
content.answer_block: Monetizing dev skills requires moving from billable hours to productized SaaS assets. By leveraging a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you compress months of boilerplate development into days. Using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for autonomous implementation, you can ship premium, production-ready applications that command high recurring monthly revenue.
content.intro:
The freelance trap is real: you trade time for money, working until you burn out, only to find your bank account stagnant. You possess the architecture skills, but the friction of building from scratch keeps you trapped in the 'service provider' lane while the SaaS owners capture all the value.

It is time to pivot. By adopting a hardened, repeatable SaaS starter kit, you stop reinventing the wheel and start deploying assets. We are moving beyond simple code snippets; we are building an assembly line that turns your technical expertise into a scalable software empire.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation. We define the database schema, auth flow, and payment integration requirements before writing a single line of application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS architecture documentation including Supabase for auth/DB, Stripe for billing, and Next.js 15 App Router structure. Focus on modularity for multi-tenant data isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding based on the Antigravity architecture spec'

steps.title: Implement Multi-tenant Auth
steps.description: Security is non-negotiable. Use pre-configured auth hooks to ensure every user belongs to a specific organization, preventing data leakage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure middleware implementation for Next.js that handles multi-tenancy based on subdomains or organization IDs, integrated with Supabase Auth.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement auth middleware and organization-based row-level security policies'

steps.title: Stripe Billing Integration
steps.description: Automate your revenue. Implement webhooks to handle subscription states, upgrades, and cancellations without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a Stripe billing flow. Include webhook handlers for subscription.created, subscription.updated, and subscription.deleted events to sync with the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Stripe API integration and webhook endpoint listeners'

steps.title: Rapid CRUD Module Generation
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the core business features of your app, ensuring consistent coding patterns across all modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable CRUD component pattern that works with React Hook Form and Zod validation, designed for rapid feature expansion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create generic CRUD scaffold for the main resources module'

steps.title: Automated Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Ensure your code ships seamlessly. Configure CI/CD to run tests and push to production only when the build is stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a GitHub Actions workflow for Vercel deployment that includes ESLint, TypeScript compilation checks, and unit test execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup CI/CD pipeline for automated production deployment'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'feature flags' from day one to deploy code to production without exposing unfinished modules to clients.
Always use Zod for runtime type checking; it acts as both documentation and a defensive shield against malformed API data.
Implement a centralized 'event bus' early to decouple your business logic from your UI components for easier maintenance.
Use SQLite in development for speed, but ensure your Drizzle or Prisma schema is strictly Postgres-compatible for production parity.
Never hardcode pricing tiers; fetch them directly from Stripe metadata so you can pivot your pricing strategy without pushing new code.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is deployed, focus on building one 'killer feature' that differentiates your product from generic templates, then launch to a closed beta list.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for clients and start building for your future. With this starter kit, you have the leverage to ship products faster than any agency in the game. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
